title: The Bus Driver Who Waves
summary: Reflect on a bus driver you see every day and what their small gesture means to you, using present simple.
topic: The bus driver I see every day
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a bus driver or another person you pass every day on your commute. Use present simple and describe what they look like, what they do, and how you feel when you see them.
sampleText: Every morning, I take the number seven bus to work. The driver is a tall man with grey hair. He always sits very still at the wheel and he looks calm. When I get on, he sometimes gives a small nod but he never says much. He wears a blue uniform and his seat is very tidy. He usually drives at a steady speed, even in rush hour. He never uses his horn unless he really needs to. I always feel safe on his bus because he is so careful. I do not know his name but I know his face very well. I recognize his shoulders and the way he holds the wheel. He sees hundreds of passengers every day but he still notices the regulars. Sometimes, when I get off the bus, he gives a small wave. That one small wave makes me feel like a person, not just a ticket.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: sits very still at the wheel
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: ngồi rất yên sau vô lăng
chỗ ngồi của ông rất gọn gàng
lái với tốc độ ổn định
biết rõ khuôn mặt của ông

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the way he holds the wheel
a person, not just a ticket
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một con người, không chỉ là một tờ vé

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for describing regular observations
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại đơn để mô tả những gì bạn thấy thường xuyên: 'He always sits', 'He usually drives', 'He never uses'. Với 'he/she/it', thêm đuôi -s/-es: 'he sits', 'he drives', 'he waves'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple to describe what you regularly observe: 'He always sits', 'He usually drives', 'He never uses'. With 'he/she/it', add -s or -es to the verb: 'he sits', 'he drives', 'he waves'.

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi sáng, tôi đi xe buýt số bảy đến chỗ làm. Người lái xe là một người đàn ông cao với mái tóc bạc. Ông luôn ngồi rất yên sau vô lăng và trông rất bình tĩnh. Khi tôi bước lên, đôi khi ông gật đầu nhẹ nhưng ông không bao giờ nói nhiều. Ông mặc đồng phục màu xanh và chỗ ngồi của ông rất gọn gàng. Ông thường lái với tốc độ ổn định, ngay cả trong giờ cao điểm. Ông không bao giờ bóp còi trừ khi thực sự cần thiết. Tôi luôn cảm thấy an toàn trên xe của ông vì ông rất cẩn thận. Tôi không biết tên ông nhưng tôi biết rõ khuôn mặt của ông. Tôi nhận ra dáng vai và cách ông cầm vô lăng. Ông nhìn thấy hàng trăm hành khách mỗi ngày nhưng vẫn để ý đến những khách quen. Đôi khi, khi tôi xuống xe, ông vẫy tay nhẹ. Một cái vẫy tay nhỏ đó khiến tôi cảm thấy như một con người, không chỉ là một tờ vé.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'He always ___ at a steady speed.'
options: drive
drives
driving
drove
mcQuestions.explanation: Third person singular present simple: 'he drives' — add -s to 'drive'.
mcQuestions.hint: What ending does the verb need with the subject 'he'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses an adverb of frequency in the correct position?
options: He usually drives carefully in rush hour.
He drives usually carefully in rush hour.
Usually drives he carefully in rush hour.
He drives carefully usually in rush hour.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'usually' goes before the main verb 'drives': 'He usually drives'.
mcQuestions.hint: The adverb of frequency goes between the subject and the main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'because' correctly to explain a feeling?
options: I feel safe because careful.
I feel safe because he is careful.
I feel safe because to be careful.
Because safe I feel he careful.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because he is careful' is a full clause with subject + verb + adjective.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because', you need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'but' to contrast knowing a face and not knowing a name?
options: I know his face and I don't know his name.
I know his face because I don't know his name.
I know his face but I don't know his name.
I know his face so I don't know his name.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' shows the contrast: you know the face well, yet you don't know the name.
mcQuestions.hint: Which connector works when two facts seem to conflict?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes someone who rarely uses the horn?
options: He always uses his horn.
He usually uses his horn.
He never uses his horn unless he needs to.
He sometimes uses his horn for fun.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'never uses his horn unless he needs to' — 'unless' adds the one exception, making it specific.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option describes someone who is calm and uses the horn only when truly needed?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the word that means 'people who travel on a bus or train':
options: drivers
passengers
workers
neighbours
mcQuestions.explanation: 'passengers' are the people who travel on public transport.
mcQuestions.hint: What do you call the people who ride a bus or train?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'still' to mean 'even so' or 'anyway'?
options: He sees hundreds of passengers but he still notices the regulars.
He sees hundreds of passengers and he still notices the regulars.
He sees hundreds of passengers still the regulars.
He sees passengers still and hundreds notices the regulars.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but he still notices the regulars' — 'still' here means 'even so', and 'but' sets up the contrast.
mcQuestions.hint: Which sentence uses both 'but' and 'still' correctly together?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which present simple sentence describes what a person looks like?
options: He weared a blue uniform.
He is wearing a blue uniform yesterday.
He wears a blue uniform.
He wear a blue uniform.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple 'he wears' correctly describes a regular appearance or work uniform.
mcQuestions.hint: Which verb form goes with 'he' in the present simple?

options: He never says much.
He says never much.
Never he says much.
He says much never.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'never' goes before the main verb: 'he never says'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'never' go in a present simple sentence?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase best describes a calm, controlled driver?
options: He drives fast and honks a lot.
He drives at a steady speed and stays calm.
He drives slowly because he is lost.
He drives and shouts at other drivers.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'steady speed' and 'stays calm' paint a picture of a controlled, reliable driver.
mcQuestions.hint: Pick the option that shows a driver who is careful and relaxed.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best expresses the idea that a small gesture makes you feel seen?
options: He gives a wave and I get more money.
He gives a wave and I feel angry.
He gives a wave and I feel like a person, not just a ticket.
He gives a wave and he drives away fast.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'a person, not just a ticket' — this contrast captures what a small gesture of recognition means.
mcQuestions.hint: Which sentence shows that a small act of acknowledgement has meaning?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is there someone on your daily commute or walk who always acknowledges you in a small way — a nod, a wave, a look? Does it matter to you if they do or do not?
